MemoriLabs/Memori ·GitHub ⭐ 12349 Python · AI-Memory, Enterprise-AI, Persistence-Layer, Knowledge-Graph, RAG

What it is

An infrastructure-agnostic persistence layer enabling AI agents to seamlessly accumulate and recall extremely detailed user contextual memories seamlessly avoiding database overhauls.

Problems solved

  • Shatters context amnesia inherently affecting generic LLMs executing extensive data retention architectures tracking specific behavioral patterns seamlessly lowering total active injection latency continuously.

How to get started

Employ the SDK via npm install @memorilabs/memori assigning explicitly the MEMORI_API_KEY defining attribution() functions linking explicit agent identifiers verifying exactly historical records dynamically.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

Memori functions explicitly representing an Enterprise Context Fabric specifically integrating abstract metadata attribution logic directly into generative workflows ensuring permanent chronological behavioral adaptations flawlessly.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Executing functional personalized agents demands extensive discrete RAG database architectures typically fracturing project development dynamically. Memori functions precisely abstracting entirely underlying vector stores mapping inputs securely tracking users silently retaining configurations predictably over extended chronological boundaries accurately.

3. Technical Architecture

A cloud-first API layer orchestrating highly customized Graph networks isolating variables distinctly classifying attributes mapped explicitly using unified Python/TS SDK environments connecting precisely across generic model pipelines easily.

4. Core Implementation

Routes inputs continuously resolving Entity-Process identifiers isolating session constraints evaluating real-time background heuristic extractions appending relationship models effectively augmenting prompt contexts preemptively assuring maximum LLM fidelity perfectly bypassing manual context injection requirements natively.

5. Code Organization

Encapsulated distinctly maintaining core client wrappers formatting HTTP abstractions properly validating specific Model Context Protocol instructions connecting seamlessly native CLI execution tools evaluating memory allocations systematically.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

Integrate Memori purely running the MCP protocol defining exactly --header "X-Memori-Process-Id: claude-code" bridging local terminal instructions accurately allowing native memory persistence reliably avoiding code implementations entirely.

7. Summary

A profoundly seamless persistence middleware accelerating heavily personalized agent integrations dramatically lowering technical constraints establishing permanent context logic predictably maximizing overall generative satisfaction infinitely.
